title: papaver radicatum subsp. alaskanum
intro: Papaver radicatum subsp. alaskanum, also known as the Alaskan poppy or Alaska papaver, is a subspecies of the Papaver radicatum plant that is native to Alaska and parts of Canada. This plant is a perennial member of the poppy family and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Papaver radicatum subsp. alaskanum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be applied topically or ingested under the guidance of a medical professional.
